holdings.infoBox: WCLD is an equity ETF with a total of 64 individual holdings. The top holdings are DigitalOcean Holdings stock at 3.81%, JFrog at 2.89%, Datadog at 2.81%, Palo Alto Networks at 2.60%, and Twilio at 2.53%.

overview.description: The WisdomTree Cloud Computing Fund (WCLD) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the BVP Nasdaq Emerging Cloud index. The fund tracks and index of US companies primarily focused on cloud software and services. Stocks are equal weighted in the index. WCLD was launched on Sep 6, 2019 and is issued by WisdomTree.

data.title: Europe unveils tech sovereignty package amid growing concerns over reliance on U.S. tech
data.text: The proposals include a new act to bolster advanced chip manufacturing and homegrown cloud computing.

data.title: SaaS-pocalypse Reversal? AI Memory Chips Slide, Software Finds Strength

data.title: Software stocks are getting pulverized — but bitcoin's rebound hints that a bottom might be in
data.text: Bitcoin's relative strength on Friday may offer a bullish clue for battered software shares — that is, if a past relationship still holds.

data.title: Earnings show cloud and security software are not immune to the economic downturn
data.text: Cloud-computing companies have shown that they are not recession-proof, with many reporting either disappointing earnings or a weaker outlook in the past few days as quarterly earnings season nears it...

data.text: UiPath surged after raising its guidance, and the basket of cloud software stocks had one of its best days of a very rough year.

data.text: Sanjay Poonen, former VMware COO, joins 'TechCheck' to discuss his cloud stock predictions for 2022, why some cloud stocks will pull back and more.

data.title: Cloud stocks are off to a brutal start to 2022 as investors sour on pandemic's top performers
data.text: The Federal Reserve's move toward raising rates is pushing investors out of cloud stocks, which soared in recent years, and in search of safer assets.
